notes: Pre-Socratic Greek philosopher known for his cosmological theories, including the explanation of the moon's illumination.

docs.title: Contra Celsum, Book 7
docs.author: Origen
docs.excerpt: … woman , a wretched slave, the wise maxims which he had learned from Anaxagoras or some other philosophers . Now if this is a true account of what constitutes the right and the wrong use of personification, have we not grounds for holding Celsus up to ridicule for thus ascribing to

docs.title: Life of Nicias
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … committed to writing how the moon is enlightened and overshadowed, was Anaxagoras; and he was as yet but recent, nor was his argument much known, but was rather kept secret, passing only amongst a few, under some kind of caution and confidence.
